response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo has over 15 years of software engineering experience spanning web, mobile, and embedded systems, but her profile is fundamentally misaligned with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and hardware-software co-design at the silicon level — none of which appear anywhere in her resume. Her performance engineering exposure is limited to web-scale HTTP throughput and low-latency network I/O using D-lang async primitives, which does not translate to deep-learning workload analysis or architecture-level bottleneck identification. There is no evidence of GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or neural network inference work, and her educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no graduate-level engineering credentials) falls short of the role's stated requirements.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse engineering experience including low-latency, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, demonstrating some performance-oriented engineering instincts
Exposure to hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms in D/Java at Altom; wave-field synthesis with C++ for bachelor thesis) shows cursory familiarity with systems below the application layer
Current AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) indicates awareness of the AI tooling landscape, even if not at the hardware level
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary technical requirement of the role is entirely absent from the resume across all 15+ years of employment history
No deep-learning workload expertise, GPU architecture experience, or SIMD/MIMD background; the closest AI work is application-layer MCP tooling, not hardware optimization

Educational credentials (two Bachelor's degrees; no Master's or PhD) combined with a web/full-stack career trajectory do not meet the technical depth expected for a Principal-level hardware architecture IC

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or AI accelerator experience
No deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
